title: Model for a Quantum Hall Effect without Landau Levels: Condensed-Matter Realization of the 'Parity Anomaly'
canonical_scope: External source anchor for the lattice parity-anomaly realization and Haldane-model branch in the topological-phases core network.

section_map.title: Main Result
section_map.summary: A lattice model realizes quantized Hall response without Landau levels and interprets the result through the parity anomaly.

section_map.title: Lattice Model With Complex Next-Nearest-Neighbor Hopping
section_map.summary: The honeycomb lattice acquires a time-reversal-breaking perturbation through complex next-nearest-neighbor hopping while keeping zero net magnetic flux per unit cell.

section_map.title: Dirac-Mass Criterion At K And K Prime
section_map.summary: The two graphene Dirac cones acquire masses controlled by the staggered potential and the complex next-nearest-neighbor phase; the sign pattern determines whether the phase is topological.

section_map.title: Chern-Insulator Phase Without Landau Levels
section_map.summary: Opposite effective mass signs at the two valleys produce a nonzero Chern number and quantized Hall response without an external magnetic field.

equation_map.label: complex-nnn-hopping-term
equation_map.title: Complex next-nearest-neighbor hopping that breaks time reversal

equation_map.label: dirac-mass-at-k-and-k-prime
equation_map.title: Valley masses m_K and m_K prime from staggered potential and hopping phase

equation_map.label: chern-number-from-valley-masses
equation_map.title: Chern number as the signed sum of the two valley contributions
